topic: Natural Language Processing: Transformers, Attention, and Tokenization

questions.scenario: A leading e-commerce company wants to develop a chatbot that can understand and respond to customer queries in real-time, handling a large volume of conversations simultaneously. The chatbot will be trained on a large dataset of customer interactions, but the dataset is highly skewed, with a large majority of queries related to order tracking and returns.
questions.question: What would be the most appropriate technique to handle the skewed dataset and improve the chatbot's performance on less frequent query types?
options: C) Using class weights to adjust the loss function
D) Using transfer learning with a pre-trained language model
questions.correct_answer_text: C) Using class weights to adjust the loss function
questions.explanation: Using class weights to adjust the loss function is a common technique to handle class imbalance in datasets. By assigning higher weights to the less frequent classes, the model is incentivized to perform better on those classes, which can improve overall performance and reduce the impact of the skew.

questions.scenario: A language translation model using the Transformer architecture is being developed to translate text from English to Spanish. However, the model is struggling to capture long-range dependencies in the input text, resulting in poor translation quality.
questions.question: What modification to the Transformer architecture would be most likely to improve the model's ability to capture long-range dependencies?
options: A) Increasing the number of attention heads
B) Increasing the dimensionality of the input embeddings
C) Adding recurrent neural network (RNN) layers to the model
D) Using a different attention mechanism, such as hierarchical attention
questions.correct_answer_text: A) Increasing the number of attention heads
questions.explanation: The Transformer architecture relies on self-attention mechanisms to capture dependencies between different parts of the input sequence. Increasing the number of attention heads allows the model to capture a wider range of dependencies and can improve its ability to capture long-range dependencies.

questions.scenario: A sentiment analysis model is being developed to analyze customer reviews of products. The model uses a tokenization approach that splits text into individual words, but it is struggling to handle out-of-vocabulary (OOV) words and typos.
questions.question: What tokenization approach would be most suitable for handling OOV words and typos in this scenario?
options: A) Word-level tokenization with a dictionary-based approach
B) Subword-level tokenization using WordPiece or BPE
D) Using a pre-trained language model with a fixed vocabulary
questions.correct_answer_text: B) Subword-level tokenization using WordPiece or BPE
questions.explanation: Subword-level tokenization approaches, such as WordPiece or BPE, can handle OOV words and typos more effectively than word-level tokenization. These approaches split words into subwords, which can capture the meaning of OOV words and typos, and can also reduce the vocabulary size.
